items.title: Apple Maps may be about to get ads
items.content: Apple Maps may soon serve ads in top results when users search on terms like "restaurants."

items.title: Someone has publicly leaked an exploit kit that can hack millions of iPhones
items.content: Leaked "DarkSword" exploits published to GitHub allow hackers and cybercriminals to target iPhone users running old versions of iOS with spyware, according to cybersecurity re

items.title: Apple sets June date for WWDC 2026, teasing ‘AI advancements’
items.content: Apple will host its next Worldwide Developers Conference the week of June 8. The company is expected to announce major updates to Siri with advanced AI capabilities.

items.title: Russians caught stealing personal data from Ukrainians with new advanced iPhone hacking tools
items.content: A suspected group of Russian government hackers was caught targeting Ukrainians with new iPhone hacking tools designed for espionage and potentially to steal crypto.

items.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
items.content: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

items.title: Microsoft hires the team of Sequoia-backed AI collaboration platform, Cove
items.content: AI collaboration startup Cove is shutting down after its team joined Microsoft, with service ending April 1 and customer data set for deletion.

items.title: The billion-dollar infrastructure deals powering the AI boom
items.content: Here's everything we know about the biggest AI infrastructure projects, including major spending from Meta, Oracle, Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI.

items.title: The White House wants AI companies to cover rate hikes. Most have already said they would.
items.content: Many hyperscalers have already made public commitments to cover electricity cost increases.

items.title: War in Iran Is Disrupting Air Travel, Hitting Middle East Hardest
items.content: Some airlines and travelers, especially in the Middle East and elsewhere in Asia, are bearing the brunt of the pain, while others are doing better.

items.title: Investors Question Whether Quick Peace in Iran Is Possible
items.content: Global markets are under pressure again amid doubts that the fighting in the Middle East can end anytime soon.

items.title: College Graduates Are Facing the Grimmest Job Market in Years
items.content: Artificial intelligence could reshape work, but for now a low-hire, low-fire labor market is the main impediment for young people seeking employment.

items.title: Reality TV Confronts a Harsh TV Reality
items.content: The number of unscripted series has plummeted by a third since 2022. As the industry rapidly changes, an era is quietly vanishing.
